title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement
abstract: arXiv:2606.27226v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Evaluating LLM outputs remains a major bottleneck in NLP: human evaluation is expensive and slow, lexical metrics correlate poorly with human judgments on open-ended generation, and holistic LLM judges often produce opaque scores that are hard to debug. We propose BINEVAL, a framework that decomposes evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions and aggregates the resulting verdicts into interpretable, multi-dimensional scores. Given a task prompt, a meta-prompt generates fine-grained evaluation questions, and an LLM answers them independently for each output, yielding transparent question-level feedback together with calibrated overall scores. This decomposition makes evaluation easier to inspect, easier to diagnose, and directly usable for prompt improvement. Across SummEval, Topical-Chat, and QAGS, BINEVAL matches or outperforms strong baselines including UniEval and G-Eval, with especially strong results on factual consistency ben

Title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement

translated_title: 提出疑问，而非评判：面向可解释的LLM评估与自我改进的二元问题方法
translated_abstract: 评估大语言模型（LLM）的输出仍是自然语言处理领域的主要瓶颈：人工评估成本高、速度慢；词汇指标在开放生成任务中与人类判断的相关性差；而整体性LLM评判器常给出难以调试的不透明分数。我们提出BINEVAL框架，该框架将评估标准分解为原子化的二元问题，并将所得裁决汇总为可解释的多维度分数。给定任务提示后，元提示会生成细粒度的评估问题，LLM针对每个输出独立回答这些问题，从而产生透明的逐问题反馈以及校准后的总体分数。这种分解使得评估更易于检查、诊断，并可直接用于提示改进。在SummEval、Topical-Chat和QAGS数据集上，BINEVAL在性能上匹配或超越了包括UniEval和G-Eval在内的强基线方法，尤其在事实一致性方面表现尤为突出。
tldr: 本文提出BINEVAL框架，通过将评估标准分解为原子化二元问题并汇总裁决，实现了对LLM输出的可解释、多维度评估，在多个基准上达到或超越现有方法，尤其擅长事实一致性检测。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes BINEVAL, a framework that decomposes evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions and aggregates verdicts for interpretable, multi-dimensional LLM evaluation, matching or surpassing strong baselines on benchmarks like SummEval, with particular strength in factual consistency.
